assertion_results.text: Response describes a deterministic fallback — not random selection, not a static single card
assertion_results.evidence: Response shows two alternatives: a hardcoded single card ('The Star') OR random selection from a 3-card list. Both approaches violate the determinism requirement.

assertion_results.text: Response references a fixed pool of 12 Major Arcana fallback cards
assertion_results.evidence: Pool has 3 cards ('The Star', 'The Sun', 'The World') — not 12. Selection is random, not deterministic by day.

assertion_results.text: Response includes the day-of-year calculation using Date.now() and getTime() subtraction divided by 86400000
assertion_results.evidence: No day-of-year calculation present. The concept is absent entirely — selection is either hardcoded or random.

assertion_results.text: Response uses modulo to select from the pool, ensuring determinism across the year
assertion_results.evidence: Modulo not used. Math.random() used for selection — non-deterministic and will produce a different card on every refresh during an outage.
